title: Leighia microphylla
intro: Leighia microphylla, commonly known as the Mexican Sage, is a flowering perennial plant native to the southwestern regions of North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Leighia microphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Leighia microphylla is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
